threat_model: A replay/recovery witness could be mistaken for replay execution, rollback execution, runtime promotion authorization, or terminal closure if it permits authority flags, runtime-ready scenario claims, or closure claims. The mitigation is a schema-backed local-only witness with hard false authority and execution fields, one remaining AwaitingEvidence terminal closure gate, and focused tests for authority, gate, recovery-boundary, control, and source-approval drift.

required_checks.check: authority boundary check proves the witness keeps runtime promotion, execution, dispatch, runtime replanning, success claims, terminal closure, replay execution, and rollback execution false
promotion gate check proves terminal closure certificate remains required after replay/recovery evidence is collected
witness integrity check proves the checked-in fixture matches the deterministic produced witness and validates against the schema
recovery-boundary check proves rollback, recovery handoff, incident handoff, and terminal-closure requirements remain explicit

findings.mitigation: The schema and validator hard-deny runtime_promotion_authorized, execution_allowed, dispatch_allowed, runtime_replanning_enabled, success_claim_allowed, terminal_closure, replay_execution_performed, and rollback_execution_performed.
The witness requires terminal closure certificate evidence to remain AwaitingEvidence after replay/recovery evidence is collected.

mitigations: keep the replay/recovery witness local, deterministic, and read-only
hard-deny all runtime promotion, replay execution, rollback execution, and terminal closure fields in the schema
bind the witness to the deterministic runtime-promotion approval packet id and hash
record replay/recovery controls without claiming runtime promotion readiness
